title: Lastrea urens
intro: Lastrea urens, also known as the prickly fern, is a species of fern native to Asia, characterized by its dark green, lance-shaped fronds with sharp, toothed margins. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea urens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lastrea urens include tea infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, fever, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Lastrea urens is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a type of lady fern and is native to various regions, including Asia and parts of Europe. This species is often classified under the genus Blechnum in some sources, but the classification can vary. The specific classification of Lastrea urens may change based on ongoing research and taxonomic updates. Further study is needed to confirm its exact classification within the plant kingdom.
uses: Lastrea urens, which is also known as the rustyback fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
